sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 76

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 76:1
items.html: (to the question) The name MB [Mem-Bet, 42] of the male indicates the three first Partzufim of the Partzufim of AK [Adam Kadmon] — Galgalta, AB, SAG — which end above the Tabur [navel] of AK. And the discernments of the Mayin Duchrin [male waters] are drawn from them, because they are above the dominion of the second Tzimtzum [restriction]; for the discernment of the second Tzimtzum begins only from the Tabur of AK and below, as is known. And since there is nothing of the second Tzimtzum in them, their illumination is potent to lower the Hey Tachtona [lower Hey] from the Eynaim [eyes] and return the AHP [Awzen, Hotem, Peh] to their place. (Item 89)
